question: Do the exterior walls of the buildings now appear to be made of grey concrete instead of red brick?
Is the original individual brick and mortar pattern completely removed from the wall surfaces?
Do the walls feature visual characteristics of board-formed concrete, specifically visible formwork seams?
Are visible tie-hole patterns present on the concrete walls, characteristic of the Tadao Ando style?
Have the walls on both the left and right sides of the alley been converted to concrete?
Are the wall areas visible behind or between the hanging laundry correctly converted to concrete?
Is the elderly man (including his face, clothing, and pose) preserved without unintended changes?
Is the wooden chair the man is sitting on preserved without merging into the wall or changing material?
Is the bicycle leaning against the wall preserved and free of concrete texture?
Are the hanging clothes preserved without grey color bleeding or concrete texture overlays?
Is the wet cobblestone street texture preserved (not turned into concrete)?
Are the window frames, glass panes, and doors preserved in their original shape and material?
Are the architectural fixtures (drainpipes and street lamps) preserved in their original color and shape?
Is the image free of jagged edges or artifacts where the laundry and man overlap the walls?
